title: 控方证人
summary:
Sir Arlington invites his nephew, Dermot, to his study, where he confronts him regarding Dermot's feelings for Claire Trent. Sir Arlington warns Dermot that Claire is unsuitable, revealing that he is attending the evening's dinner in a professional capacity because he believes Claire is mentally ill. He cites a family history of mental illness on the mother's side and argues that Claire must be restrained to prevent a "deadly form of murderous mania." 

Dermot rejects his uncle's medical assessment and declares his love for Claire, vowing to protect her from interfering doctors. Sir Arlington threatens to disinherit Dermot and leave his property to hospitals if Dermot attempts to take Claire away. During their heated argument, the servant Johnson enters with drinks, overhearing part of the dispute. Dermot eventually apologizes for his tone but maintains his stance, stating that money cannot influence his feelings, before exiting the house and taking a taxi to the Grafton Gallery.

At the gallery, Dermot finds Claire and asks her to dance. When they move to a quiet corner, Claire confesses that she has fallen in love with him, but she urges him to leave because she believes it is "too late" for them to be together. When Dermot suggests fleeing to the South Pacific to be safe, Claire breaks free from his embrace, crying that she cannot be with him because of a sense of shame.
